w.label: the shallow end · the water is 62°F · Ford said so

W.label: the deep end · counselors say no · Ford says the spring feeds from the north

T.label: an alder tree · the pond gets its name from these

R.label: a boulder · Bea would want to sit on this

S.label: the swimming zone sign · beginners left, advanced right

H.label: a great blue heron standing very still · looks at you and looks away

s.label: the swim area · press space to swim (advances time)

c.label: the racked canoes · press space to canoe (advances time)

b.label: the boathouse · locked · Wilson has the key · or Reggie's dad does

1.label: an unbroken white cockle shell on the dock

2.label: a used Nikon lens cap in the grass under the alders

notes: Alder Pond · morning activity zone for swimming and canoes.  Two swim spots on the west edge (marked s), a rack of canoes east of that (c), a boulder Bea would want, an alder grove around the perimeter, and a great blue heron at the northwest edge.  The boathouse is bottom-right · locked at Wave G · Wave K unlocks it via Reggie's KNACK or Nika's SNEAK.  The path back to camp is bottom-left (p).
